title: Robust Vietnamese Mackerel Banh Cuon
description: Mustardy, gooey rolls of steamed rice flour sheets filled with seared mackerel and fresh raw veggies, perfect for a post-workout boost.
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp sesame oil in a pan over medium heat and sear the mackerel fillet until cooked through, about 5 minutes per side.
In a small bowl whisk together rice flour, tapioca flour, ground mustard seeds, soy sauce, fish sauce, sugar, lime juice, and chili flakes to form a smooth batter.
Separate the pandan leaves and set aside.
Pour the batter onto a steaming tray or a shallow pan, cover with foil, and steam for 4 minutes until the sheet is set but still pliable.
Remove the steamed sheet and lay it flat on a clean work surface.
Place a quarter of the seared mackerel, a handful of shredded veggies, and a drizzle of remaining sauce in the center of the sheet.
Fold the edges of the sheet over the filling to create a tight roll, then wrap the roll tightly with a pandan leaf.
Repeat the rolling process for the remaining filling and sheets.
Serve the rolls warm, optionally with extra lime wedges and a side of fresh herbs.
Enjoy the creamy, mustardy flavors as a satisfying post-workout meal.
